title: Adams abilities
wikitext:
[]: Adam's abilities             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Aug 22, 2008 9:14 pm Personally, I think Adam is one of the most underappreciated Angels. However, that's beside the point. What I'd like to know is if it's a safe assumption that he had the same powers as one of the Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 22, 2008 9:24 pm I would guess he would be comparable to S^2 equipped Unit 01. Unfortunately, we don't see a whole lot of S^2 equipped Unit 01. So ultimately, the question is probably a yes, but who knows?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Aug 23, 2008 12:13 am Yes, I would say the same thing as FO.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sat Aug 23, 2008 12:38 am She can kill billions of people by self destructing Edit - Nevermind. After thinking about it for a couple more seconds, this "power" (if it can be called one) is only induced after a Contact Experiment. So yeah, Adam's power is probably equivalent to that of an Eva with an S^2 Engine.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Aug 23, 2008 2:13 am Adam, I choose you. Use self destruct!

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Aug 23, 2008 10:05 am swamp147 wrote: So yeah, Adam's power is probably equivalent to that of an Eva with an S^2 Engine.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sat Aug 23, 2008 3:07 pm Yeah, a God. At least that's what Rtsuko was saying in the Reiquarium at the end of episode 23.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Aug 23, 2008 4:07 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: she has both "Fruits" inside her.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:59 pm I thought it would be S2-equipped Unit 02.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 30, 2008 11:16 pm Under not-so-pressing circumstances, Adam could wipe out all Lilith-based life with her 1337 AATF and even do some terraforming, which are basic features all Seeds probably have. In hand-to-hand combat, Adam isn't quite as effectively bitchy as Lilith and lacks the effective "marshmallowy flesh absorbs all hits that contact" feature, which is why she lost the duel that never happened. Not even her down-B move, whereby she spontaneously squirts out an egg that can be employed as a weapon, could save her. Her Final Smash is also pretty risky, since it involves her S2 Engine going insane and eventually blowing up, with no guarantee that she'll take down her opponent before the move ends. I could have developed this joke further, but it's late. You understand.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Sep 01, 2008 3:31 am It seems that the Seeds are considerably stronger versions of the Evas/Angels, given their uber-S2 abilities and the whole birthing thing. Fazmotron wrote: Adam, I choose you. Use self destruct!

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Sep 01, 2008 4:56 am Adam maybe the enbodiment of self-sacrifice. Maybe we have somekind of homage to the Kamikaze attacks here...

[]:             Quote Post by Caiden » Mon Sep 01, 2008 10:42 am Yeah, Adam is pretty much Pwnage in a bottle. I would say it would be much like Eva 03 with the angel inside of it. But better, yah kno.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 01, 2008 1:04 pm The Imperialist wrote: Adam maybe the enbodiment of self-sacrifice. Maybe we have somekind of homage to the Kamikaze attacks here...

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Fri Sep 05, 2008 8:22 pm Reichu wrote: Under not-so-pressing circumstances, Adam could wipe out all Lilith-based life with her 1337 AATF and even do some terraforming, which are basic features all Seeds probably have. In hand-to-hand combat, Adam isn't quite as effectively bitchy as Lilith and lacks the effective "marshmallowy flesh absorbs all hits that contact" feature, which is why she lost the duel that never happened. Not even her down-B move, whereby she spontaneously squirts out an egg that can be employed as a weapon, could save her. Her Final Smash is also pretty risky, since it involves her S2 Engine going insane and eventually blowing up, with no guarantee that she'll take down her opponent before the move ends. I could have developed this joke further, but it's late. You understand.

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Sat Sep 06, 2008 11:53 pm Zaiku wrote: Evangelion + Brawl = win

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Sep 06, 2008 11:58 pm That game's awesome, I'm thinking of buying it on eBay.

[]:             Quote Post by evilhead008 » Sun Sep 07, 2008 8:44 pm That game's awesome, I'm thinking of buying it on eBay.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Sep 07, 2008 8:50 pm Looks awesome. I've watched all the game play videos. You can find a torrent of the game, it's about 300 MB. And a PS2 emulator is not hard to find with BIOs. And it's supposed to be like SSB. If they wanted to just recreate battles or use Eva physics.... Well that's what Eva 64 is for.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Nov 03, 2008 8:39 pm What if Adam exhibited the powers of his children? Those beam attacks, shapeshifting and evolutionary abilities had to come from somewhere

Article: Adams abilities
[]: Adam's abilities             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Aug 22, 2008 9:14 pm Personally, I think Adam is one of the most underappreciated Angels. However, that's beside the point. What I'd like to know is if it's a safe assumption that he had the same powers as one of the Evas.

After thinking about it for a couple more seconds, this "power" (if it can be called one) is only induced after a Contact Experiment. So yeah, Adam's power is probably equivalent to that of an Eva with an S^2 Engine.
